title: FamilyRelationships
lean_explanation:
The LN block introduces, for a CDMG $G = (J, V, E, L)$, eight family-relationship operators: parents, children, siblings, ancestors, descendants, non-descendants, strongly connected components, and districts. The Lean encoding emits one `def` per LN item, plus a set-form `def` (`PaSet`, `ChSet`, `AncSet`, `DescSet`, `ScSet`, `DistSet`) for the six operators where the LN writes one. `Sib` has no set form and `NonDesc` has no per-vertex form, mirroring the LN's asymmetric presentation.

`G : CDMG Node` is the ambient CDMG over a vertex type `Node : Type*` — the silent carrier the LN assumes when it writes $v \in J \cup V$. `v : Node` is the focal vertex; `A : Set Node` is the focal subset. We do not pin `v` or `A` to $G$ on the function argument: every set-builder body conjoins `w ∈ G` on the output side (via the `Membership` instance from `CDMGNotation`), so out-of-graph inputs harmlessly return the empty set rather than forcing an extra hypothesis at the call site.

Non-trivial Mathlib idioms in play. `Set Node` is the powerset carrier, used uniformly across the family — a `Finset` carrier was deliberately rejected (see design notes). `⋃ v ∈ A, G.Pa v` desugars to `Set.biUnion`, exposing Mathlib's full biUnion API (`Set.biUnion_union`, `Set.biUnion_singleton`, `Set.subset_biUnion_of_mem`) — every LN algebraic identity on set forms lifts through this API for free. `((G.J ∪ G.V : Finset Node) : Set Node) \ G.DescSet A` coerces the `Finset` union $J \cup V$ to a `Set` and takes Mathlib's set-difference; the coercion form lifts every `Finset` lemma about $J \cup V$ to `Set` automatically via `Finset.coe_union`. `Set.inter` is the canonical Mathlib intersection.

The walk-based operators (`Anc`, `Desc`, `Dist`) introduce an existential over `Walk G _ _` paired with a `Prop`-predicate `IsDirectedWalk` or `IsBidirectedWalk` from `def_3_4`. The walk inductive carries the data (vertex list, intermediate edges) and the flavour is a predicate on top of the same carrier, so concatenation, reversal, and restriction lemmas lift uniformly across the family. Self-membership $v \in \mathrm{Anc}^G(v)$ and its analogues are witnessed by the length-$0$ walk `Walk.nil v hv`, on which both predicates evaluate to `True`.
design_choices:
**`Set Node` carrier, not `Finset Node`.** Three of the eight operators (`Anc`, `Desc`, `Dist`) are defined by *existence of a walk*, which is not immediately decidable on a general CDMG — the walk inductive admits arbitrary lengths even on a finite vertex set. A `Finset` carrier would have demanded a `Decidable` instance for walk existence at the definition site, forcing every downstream consumer to thread that instance through. `Set Node` defers this to the point of use; finiteness lemmas — every family set is contained in the finite $J \cup V$ — are proved separately as the chapter needs them.

**Per-vertex form is primitive; set form is `Set.biUnion`.** The LN consistently builds set forms by union over per-vertex forms. Mirroring this layering means downstream proofs lift the LN's algebraic identities ($\mathrm{Pa}(A \cup B) = \mathrm{Pa}(A) \cup \mathrm{Pa}(B)$, monotonicity, singleton collapse) directly via Mathlib's biUnion API. The reverse layering would force every per-vertex use site to peel off a singleton biUnion.

**Self-loop convention for `Pa` and `Ch`: `v ∈ Pa G v` when a directed self-loop is present.** The LN explicitly considered and rejected the alternative $\mathrm{Pa}^G(v) := \{w \mid (w, v) \in E\} \setminus \{v\}$ precisely so that the singleton identity $\mathrm{Pa}^G(\{v\}) = \mathrm{Pa}^G(v)$ holds. Patching out `v` would break that identity. `Sib G v`, in contrast, *cannot* contain `v` — the CDMG axiom `hL_irrefl` rules out bidirected self-loops a priori. The asymmetry is graph-theoretic, not definitional.

**Walk-based operators reuse `def_3_4`'s `Walk` plus a `Prop`-predicate.** Re-using the single `Walk` inductive (rather than introducing separate `DirectedWalk` / `BidirectedWalk` types) means every case-analysis lemma proved for plain walks lifts to the directed and bidirected flavours via the predicate hypothesis. `Relation.TransGen G.tuh` was considered for reachability and rejected: it loses the walk-as-data needed downstream for paths and m-separation. Self-membership $v \in \mathrm{Anc}^G(v)$, $v \in \mathrm{Desc}^G(v)$, $v \in \mathrm{Sc}^G(v)$, $v \in \mathrm{Dist}^G(v)$ holds unconditionally because `Walk.nil v hv` is a legal length-$0$ walk and both predicates evaluate to `True` on `nil`; no $\cup \{v\}$ patch sits on top of the walk body.

**`Sib` has no set form; `NonDesc` has no per-vertex form.** The LN's block is intentionally asymmetric — $\mathrm{Sib}^G(A)$ and $\mathrm{NonDesc}^G(v)$ are not written. The Lean encoding mirrors that asymmetry; consumers who need them write `⋃ v ∈ A, Sib G v` or `NonDesc G {v}` locally.

**`NonDesc`'s complement is taken inside $J \cup V$.** Taking it inside `V` alone would wrongly exclude input nodes (which the LN considers non-descendants); taking it inside `Set.univ` would wrongly include every node of the ambient `Node` type. The coercion form `((G.J ∪ G.V : Finset Node) : Set Node)` lifts `Finset` lemmas about $J \cup V$ to `Set` automatically.

**`Sc G v := Anc G v ∩ Desc G v`, the literal LN formula.** Using `Set.inter` reuses the existentials already inside `Anc` and `Desc`, instead of forcing a fresh existential pair witness at every use site. The note $v \in \mathrm{Sc}^G(v)$ is then a one-line corollary via `Set.mem_inter` — not a new axiom.
